about.intro.title: Attitude Before Garment
about.intro.sub: the attitude comes first. the clothes are just where it lands.
about.chapter1.title: the name
about.chapter1.text1: ABG means Attitude Before Garment. You don't put on a piece to build a personality. You already have one. The piece just doesn't get in the way of it.
about.chapter1.text2: Most clothing is trying to tell you something. A logo saying look how much this cost, a graphic saying look at me. ABG isn't trying to say anything. It's for people who already know what they like and would rather not explain it.
about.chapter2.title: the cut
about.chapter2.text1: What we obsess over is the cut: pattern, proportion, how a piece sits and moves. Not the print. If we make a t-shirt, it's not a canvas for a graphic. It's a genuinely well-cut t-shirt, full stop.
about.chapter2.text2: Premium construction. Considered fit. No gatekept price tag. Comfortable enough that no one clocks you tried, cut well enough that someone who looks twice can tell. And you'll never confirm that to them.
about.chapter3.title: who it's for
about.chapter3.text1: People who formed their taste before anyone told them to, and are still quietly improving it without ever letting you see them try.
about.chapter3.text2: They don't check what's trending before deciding what they like. They'd rather keep growing than stay in one place. What they're terrified of isn't effort. It's being caught performing it.
about.chapter4.title: how we drop
about.chapter4.text1: Limited runs. Urgency from real numbers and real dates, not hype language. We state what we made, when it ends, and leave it at that.
about.chapter4.text2: We built our own checkout. When a drop goes live, it's clean. No chasing, no chaos.
about.chapter4.footer: the garment should speak through how it's made, not what's printed on it.
